data.title: A whale, having set 10 major targets, holds $156 million worth of long BTC positions, currently showing a floating profit of $5.52 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the whale "First Set 10 Big Targets" has opened a long position of 2250.15 BTC, worth $156 million, with an average opening price of $67193.72 and a liquidation price of $55423, currently showing a floating profit of $5.52 million.

data.title: Institutions: Cooling expectations of interest rate cuts and the anticipated end of the Middle East conflict may limit the upside potential for gold prices.
data.description: Daniel Takieddine, CEO of Sky Links Capital, stated that gold's upside potential may be limited as market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut weaken. Strong US labor market data supported US Treasury yields, putting pressure on gold. Continued diplomatic efforts supported market expectations of a potential resolution to the Middle East conflict, further increasing uncertainty surrounding the gold outlook. However, geopolitical risks outside the Middle East and continued gold purchases by central banks are still providing support for gold prices. He pointed out that gold's short-term movement will depend on upcoming US economic data, Federal Reserve policy signals, and developments in the geopolitical situation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Surge Boosts Whale's Profit to Over $820,000
data.description: A whale investor, known as "Set 10 Big Goals," has seen their floating profit surpass $820,000 following Bitcoin's rise above $70,000. According to NS3.AI, the whale had previously opened a long position of at least 278.406 BTC at a price of $67,023.8, amounting to approximately $18.66 million.

data.title: Brent crude oil fell 2% on the day, currently trading at $104.29 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, Brent crude oil fell by 2% during the day, currently trading at $104.29 per barrel.

data.title: Whale's BTC long position, which "sets 10 big targets first," may have unrealized profits exceeding $820,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, as BTC broke through $70,000, the whale "First Set 10 Big Targets" previously opened a long position of at least 278,406 BTC at $67,023.8, worth approximately $18.66 million, and is currently showing a floating profit of $820,000.

data.title: Iran says US threats to strike Iranian infrastructure constitute "war crimes".
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Bagheei condemned the US threats against Iranian infrastructure as "war crimes" and warned that any country assisting Washington would be held legally accountable. He said, "There is no doubt that simply issuing such threats is a war crime," noting that the US talks about attacking "energy and industrial infrastructure" while simultaneously "giving Israel the green light to attack civilian targets." He emphasized that these actions constitute war crimes under international humanitarian law and the Statute of the International Criminal Court. "All countries should understand that any act of cooperation or assistance provided by the US in committing its crimes must be held accountable and should be recognized as a war crime and a crime against humanity." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Iran Condemns U.S. Threats Against Infrastructure as War Crimes
data.description:
On April 6, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Ismail Baghaei condemned U.S. threats against Iran's infrastructure, labeling them as 'war crimes.' According to BlockBeats, Baghaei warned that any nation assisting Washington would bear legal responsibility. He stated, 'There is no doubt that merely issuing such threats constitutes a war crime,' highlighting the U.S.'s contradictory stance of targeting 'energy and industrial infrastructure' while permitting Israel to attack civilian targets.

Baghaei emphasized that these actions are considered war crimes under international humanitarian law and the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court. He urged all countries to recognize that any cooperation or assistance in the U.S.'s criminal activities must be held accountable and deemed as war crimes and crimes against humanity.

data.title: Brent Crude Oil Experiences 2% Daily Decline
data.description: Brent crude oil prices have seen a 2% drop, currently trading at $104.29 per barrel. According to Odaily, this decline reflects ongoing fluctuations in the global oil market.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses 70,000 USDT Mark
data.description: Bitcoin has exceeded the 70,000 USDT threshold, currently trading at 70,042.53 USDT, marking a daily increase of 1.46%. According to Foresight News, this development reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment.

data.title: South Korea's FSC Mandates Frequent Asset Checks for Crypto Platforms
data.description: South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) has instructed local cryptocurrency exchanges to implement a system for reconciling internal ledgers with actual crypto asset holdings every five minutes by the end of May. According to Foresight News, exchanges are also required to disclose asset matching balances daily and undergo monthly external audits by accounting firms. Currently, three of the five major local crypto exchanges conduct asset checks every 24 hours, while the other two perform checks every 5 to 10 minutes. Additionally, regulators have identified deficiencies in the exchanges' circuit breaker mechanisms during significant asset mismatches.

data.title: Iran Asserts Independence Amid U.S. Peace Proposal
data.description:
Iran has finalized its demands in response to recent proposals aimed at ending the ongoing conflict, according to Odaily. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Ismail Baghaei stated that Tehran will disclose its demands at an appropriate time, emphasizing that Iran will not succumb to external pressure.

Baghaei mentioned that a 15-point plan from the United States was conveyed through intermediaries, including Pakistan and other friendly nations. He described the proposal as ambitious, unusual, and illogical, asserting that Iran has its own framework based on national interests and considerations.

He further clarified that engaging with mediators should not be interpreted as a sign of weakness. Baghaei stressed that Iran's swift and bold response to the proposal should not be seen as yielding to adversaries.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson: Iran is prepared to respond to the mediators and will provide timely updates if necessary.
data.description: An Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman said on the 6th that Iran is prepared to respond to the mediators and will provide timely updates if necessary. (Xinhua)

data.title: Iran calls the US 15-point plan "extremely ambitious and illogical."
data.description: According to the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA), Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Bagheei stated that Tehran has finalized its demands in the context of recent proposals to end the war, but will only announce them at an appropriate time, emphasizing that Iran will not succumb to pressure. He stated, "A few days ago, they put forward some proposals through intermediaries, and this 15-point US plan was conveyed through Pakistan and some other friendly countries." He added, "Such proposals are extremely ambitious, unusual, and illogical." He stressed that Iran has its own framework. "Based on our own interests and our considerations, we have compiled and formulated a series of demands that we have put forward in the past and present." He also denied that contact with mediators meant weakness. "The fact that Iran has quickly and bravely expressed its position on a proposal should not be seen as a sign of surrendering to the enemy." (Jin Shi)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Guotou Silver LOF Suspends Trading Due to Premium Concerns
data.description: On April 6, Jin10 reported that Guotou Silver LOF announced a significant premium in the secondary market trading price of its Class A fund shares compared to the net asset value. According to Jin10, to protect investor interests, the fund will suspend trading from the market opening until 10:30 on April 7, 2026, and resume trading at 10:30. Redemption operations will continue as usual during the suspension period. If the premium does not effectively decrease on April 7, the fund reserves the right to implement further suspension measures. The fund has suspended subscription operations since January 28, 2026, with the resumption date to be announced later.

data.title: Potential U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Sparks Market Debate
data.description: A report from Axios regarding a possible 45-day ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran has ignited discussions about the influence of unverified geopolitical news on market dynamics. According to NS3.AI, Reuters has stated that it could not independently confirm the ongoing negotiations. Meanwhile, Iranian officials have expressed reluctance to agree to a temporary truce without assurances of long-term commitments.

data.title: Hedge Funds Establish Largest Net Short Position in Global Equities in 13 Years
data.description: Goldman Sachs data reveals that hedge funds have established their largest net short position in global equities in 13 years as of last month. According to NS3.AI, short sales surpassed long purchases by a ratio of 7.6 to 1, with approximately 76% of these shorts focusing on index and ETF products.

data.title: Iran Ready to Respond to Mediators, Says Foreign Ministry
data.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson announced on the 6th that the country is prepared to respond to mediators if necessary. According to Odaily, the spokesperson emphasized that any required communication will be promptly conveyed.

data.title: Gold Prices Surpass $4,700 as U.S. Stock Futures Rise
data.description: Gold prices have climbed above $4,700 per ounce, marking a 0.54% increase. According to BlockBeats, this rise comes amid noticeable pre-market gains in U.S. stock futures. The Nasdaq futures have increased by 1%, while Dow Jones futures are up by 0.32%, and S&P 500 futures have risen by 0.6%.
